title: sphaeralcea grossulariifolia var. pedata
intro: Sphaeralcea grossulariifolia var. pedata, also known as the desert marigold, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high content of saponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of desert marigold include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and support the immune system. Desert marigold can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
